news.summary: A company based in San Jose, California, VSee Health, Inc. (NASDAQ: VSEE), known for its AI-powered telehealth services, is making waves in the $787 billion digital healthcare market. Their latest achievement includes the launch of the Telenursing Robotics solution, which aims to streamline nursing tasks through automation. This innovation is crucial as nursing expenses typically make up around 60% of hospital operating budgets. By offering a modular, no-code/low-code platform, VSee Health, Inc. is set to revolutionize the industry and drive down labor costs.
news.headline: VSee Health's Telenursing Robotics aims to reduce hospital nursing costs by 3-5%, targeting 60% of operating budgets.

news.summary: A partnership has been announced between Redpoint Global and HCLTech to leverage their strengths in digital engagement and customer experience technology. The collaboration aims to enhance the capabilities of both companies in these areas. HCLTech is a leading global technology company with vast experience in digital solutions, while Redpoint Global specializes in customer engagement technology. The partnership will see them working together to deliver innovative solutions to their clients. This collaboration is significant in the industry and is expected to greatly benefit both companies.
news.headline: Redpoint Global and HCLTech team up for improved engagement, ROI using data-driven experience, 3X engagement, 2X ROI.Characters: 106

news.summary: In a joint announcement from New York and Atlanta, ICE Bonds, a division of Intercontinental Exchange (NYSE: ICE), introduced a new Request-for-Quote (RFQ) protocol for Mortgage-Backed Securities (MBS). This addition to ICE Bonds' services, along with the existing MBS Click-to-Trade marketplace, enables clients to submit MBS RFQs within ICE TMC's anonymous trading pool. The enhancement provides a broader range of execution options for MBS trading on the ICE TMC platform, reinforcing ICE's position as a key player in global technology and data services.
news.headline: ICE Bonds launches new RFQ protocol for MBS on ICE TMC, enhancing trading with anonymous pool.

news.summary: MajorKey Technologies, a top identity security firm based in Chicago, has recently completed the acquisition of Oxford Computer Group LLC. This move solidifies MajorKey's position in the market and represents a significant step in their growth strategy. The acquisition is set to bring together the expertise and resources of both companies, creating a formidable force in the industry. This strategic decision is anticipated to have a substantial impact on the identity security sector, with both companies seeing a surge in their market influence and reach.
news.headline: MajorKey Technologies buys Oxford Computer Group LLC, a market leader in identity security. #Acquisition #Tech

news.summary: In 2024, the migration of U.S. residents to Florida, Texas, and other Sun Belt areas decreased notably, as reported by Redfin. According to the report, Tampa experienced a net inflow of approximately 10,000 residents, a significant drop from the 35,000-person net inflow recorded the previous year. This decline marked the most substantial slowdown in domestic migration among the top 50 populous U.S. metros. Net inflow measures the number of individuals moving into an area.
news.headline: Migration to Florida and Texas slows: Tampa's net inflow drops to 10,000 in 2024 from 35,000 in 2023, impacting homebuyer demand.

news.summary: In Richardson, Texas, there was a surge in demand for market-rate apartments during the first quarter of the year, marking the strongest Q1 performance in RealPage's 32-year history of data collection.
news.headline: RealPage reports highest Q1 demand for market-rate apartments in 32 years, predicts modest growth momentum.

news.summary: In Pittsford, New York, Greenidge Generation Holdings Inc. (Nasdaq: GREE) has appointed Kenneth Fearn and Christopher Krug as independent directors on its Board of Directors, effective April 17, 2025. Both individuals are seasoned in finance, bringing valuable expertise to the company. Mr. Fearn, a distinguished senior executive, and Mr. Krug, possess strong financial backgrounds that will benefit the Board.
news.headline: Greenidge Generation adds Kenneth Fearn and Christopher Krug to its board as independent directors, effective April 17, 2025.

news.summary: San Antonio-based Valero Energy Corporation disclosed that its subsidiary, Valero Refining Company-California, has notified the California Energy Commission about its plans to potentially idle, restructure, or halt refining activities at Valero’s Benicia Refinery by April 2026. The company, listed on the NYSE as VLO, is assessing various strategic options for its other operations in California. This decision may affect the workforce and business but is part of Valero's ongoing evaluation and future planning.
news.headline: Valero plans to idle, restructure, or cease operations at its Benicia Refinery by April 2026, impacting employees and business.

news.summary: Miami-based Paper Point Corp., a top producer of paperboard frames for pleated HVAC filters, will be showcasing its products at the renowned International Filtration Conference & Exhibition (FiltXPO) from April 29 to May 1, 2025, at the Miami Beach Convention Center. news.summary: In Bonita Springs, Florida, Herc Holdings Inc. (NYSE: HRI) has extended its tender offer to acquire all outstanding shares of H&E Equipment Services, Inc. (NASDAQ: HEES). The offer includes $78.75 in cash and 0.1287 shares of Herc common stock for each H&E share. This was pursuant to a merger agreement dated February 1. The offer is without interest.
news.headline: Herc Holdings extends tender offer for H&E Equipment Services, offering $78.75 in cash and 0.1287 shares of Herc stock per H&E share.

news.summary: Alder Packaging has announced a strategic partnership with Simex, an injection molding facility located in Medellín, Colombia. With over 55 years of experience, Simex is a well-established family-owned company. This collaboration aims to enhance Alder's sustainable packaging solutions and diversify their supply chain amidst fluctuating tariff policies. Founded in 1969, Simex is known for its expertise in manufacturing high-quality beauty products. The partnership will provide a reliable nearshore option for brands, facilitating smoother operations and ensuring continuity in the face of global supply chain disruptions.
news.headline: Alder Packaging partners with Simex for sustainable solutions; Simex: 55+yrs exp, based in Colombia, focuses on beauty.

news.summary: NÜRTINGEN, Germany - ADS-TEC Energy, a prominent player in battery-based energy storage and quick-charging systems, revealed that HyperFast, a component of the Energy Solutions Group overseeing the largest independent renewable energy production in Benelux, will utilize their ChargePost fast charging solution. news.summary: China has recently secured a strategic partnership for the supply of matcha, aimed at expanding distribution in Asia and the UAE. news.summary: A recent report revealed that there has been a significant increase in global air pollution levels in the past year. The data shows that air pollution has risen by 12% worldwide, with more than 90% of the global population living in areas that exceed the recommended air quality levels set by the World Health Organization. This increase in pollution is mainly attributed to factors such as industrial activities, transportation emissions, and wildfires. The report also highlighted that approximately 6.5 million deaths each year are linked to air pollution, making it a major public health concern.Experts are urging governments to take immediate action to combat this growing issue and reduce harmful emissions to protect the health of people and the planet.
news.headline: Vivos Therapeutics secures deal to buy Nevada's biggest sleep center operator.

news.summary: A series of tiny GLP-1 implants are being developed for chronic weight management and type 2 diabetes. These implants are designed to be inserted once or twice a year, offering a convenient and potentially more effective treatment option for patients. The portfolio includes a range of miniature implants with varying release rates and durations. This innovative approach aims to provide long-term benefits for individuals struggling with weight management and diabetes.
news.headline: Vivani Medical to join virtual fireside chat by H.C. Wainwright & Co, developing GLP-1 implants for weight management, diabetes.

news.summary: NANO Nuclear is looking to grow its engineering and project development team to bolster support for the construction and licensing endeavors of the KRONOS MMR Energy System Reactor in the United States and Canada. This expansion initiative aims to enhance the team's capabilities in managing the construction and licensing procedures for the reactor. The company is aiming to increase the team by a notable percentage, potentially hiring dozens of professionals with expertise in engineering and project development to ensure successful completion of the reactor project and secure necessary approvals for operation.
news.headline: NANO Nuclear seeks new hires to build KRONOS MMR reactors in US and Canada to support energy system.

news.summary: A recent report reveals that the unemployment rate in the United States has dropped to its lowest level in five years. The latest data shows that the rate has fallen to 4.2%, the lowest since 2016. The report also highlights an increase in job creation, with 600,000 new jobs added to the economy in the past month. The strong job market has led to a surge in consumer confidence and spending, boosting economic growth. Analysts predict that if this trend continues, the US economy is on track for a rapid recovery from the impact of the pandemic.
news.headline: Toppoint Holdings gives an update on business performance at the end of 2024, and shares growth plans.

news.summary: On April 16, 2025, Runway Growth Finance Corp. (Nasdaq: RWAY) based in Menlo Park, California, revealed plans to unveil its financial results for the first quarter of 2025 after the market closes on May 13, 2025. As a top provider of flexible capital solutions for late- and growth-stage companies looking for alternatives to equity funding, Runway Growth will host a conference call at 2:00 p.m. PT (5:00 p.m. ET) on the same day to discuss the results.
news.headline: Runway Growth Finance Corp. to announce Q1 2025 financial results on May 13, 2025, followed by a conference call at 2:00 p.m. PT.

news.summary: The US Satellite Communication (SATCOM) Equipment market is projected to grow from USD 9.59 billion in 2024 to USD 17.10 billion in 2029, indicating a CAGR of 12.2%. Key factors driving this growth include government initiatives promoting R&D in the SATCOM field and the rising need for adaptable and efficient SATCOM equipment. Major US-based SATCOM manufacturers such as General Dynamics, Honeywell International Inc., and Raytheon Intelligence and Space will significantly impact market expansion. The Space Development Agency is planning to develop a next-gen military space architecture, emphasizing small satellites in low Earth orbit (LEO), which will boost the demand for SATCOM equipment in the US. The US Department of Defense has forecasted a USD 1.70 billion contract in 2024 for notable SATCOM manufacturers, underscoring the increasing significance of SATCOM technology in military and commercial sectors. Key players in the US SATCOM industry include EchoStar Corporation, L3Harris Technologies, Inc., RTX, General Dynamics Corporation, Viasat, Inc., Honeywell International Inc., and Campbell Scientific, Inc. In terms of market segmentation, the airborne platform held the largest share in the US SATCOM Equipment market in 2024.
news.headline: US SATCOM Equipment Market to reach $17.10B by 2029, growing at 12.2% CAGR. Government initiatives and defense contracts driving the growth. Key players include General Dynamics, Honeywell, and Raytheon. Airborne segment dominates market in 2024.

news.summary: CUSIP Global Services released its CUSIP Issuance Trends Report for March 2025, showing a monthly increase in new corporate debt and equity identifiers. North American corporate CUSIP requests totaled 8,447, up 4.2% monthly but down 16.9% annually. U.S. corporate debt requests rose 2.4%, while U.S. corporate equity requests increased by 5.5%. Requests for municipal securities fell by 1.1% monthly but were up 11.4% annually. Texas led in municipal requests with 106, followed by California (104) and New York (81). International equity CUSIP requests rose by 2.6% while international debt requests fell by 5.9%. Year-to-date changes in asset classes showed a 58.7% increase in long-term municipal notes compared to 2024. International equity requests were up by 9.0% annually, and international debt requests were up by 20.5%. Director of Operations for CGS, Gerard Faulkner, noted a steady corporate issuance pace amidst economic uncertainty. Full story available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: CUSIP requests for new corporate debt and equity instruments rose in March, with North American corporate CUSIP requests totaling 8,447, up 4.2% monthly. U.S. corporate debt requests rose by 2.4% and equity requests by 5.5%. Texas led state-level municipal requests with 106 new CUSIPs. International equity CUSIP requests increased by 2.6%, while international debt requests fell by 5.9%. International equity requests were up by 9.0% annually, and international debt requests were up by 20.5% annually. Monthly total requests for municipal securities fell by 1.1%, but were up by 11.4% annually.
